pages.raw_text:
Mrs. May Arkwright Hutton,
one OF the most prominent work.
ers for equal suffrage in this state,
has returned to Spokane after a
busy season at Olympia in behalf
OF the bin which passed the sen.
ate yesterday, submitting the suf.
frage amendment to a popular
vote. Mrs. Hutton is tired but
elated, and confident that the state
constitution will be amended to
give women the right to vote. She
is highly appreciative of the aid
given her cause by members of the
Spokane delegation, especially Sen
ator Hutchinson.


Here is the senate vote on the
suffrage bill.


For-Allen, Arrasmith, Bassett,
Blair, Booth, Brown. Bryan, Cot
terill. Cox. Davis, Eastman, Fal-
coner, Fatland, Fishback, Hutchin-
son, Huxtable, Kline, Knicker-
booker, MCGOWAN, Metcalf, Myers,
Minkler. Paulhamus, Piper, Poison,
Presby, Roberts, Rosenhaupt, Stey-
enson, Stewart.


Against-Cameron. McGregor
Nichols, Potts, Ruth, Rydstrom
Smith, Smithson, Williams.


Absent-Graves, Anderson, Whit.
ney.
